questions: aquaponics ar hydroponics er difference ki?
answers: Aquaponics combines aquaculture (fish farming) with hydroponics (soilless plant growing). Fish waste provides nutrients for plants, and plants filter the water for fish — a symbiotic system.
Aquaponics holo mach chash ar hydroponics er combination. Mach er waste plants er fertilizer hoy, ar plants pani filter kore mach er jonno. Win-win system.

In aquaponics, fish produce ammonia-rich waste, bacteria convert it to nitrates, and plants absorb these nutrients. The cleaned water returns to the fish tank. It uses 90% less water than traditional farming.
To start aquaponics, you need a fish tank, grow beds, a water pump, and beneficial bacteria. Tilapia and goldfish are popular fish choices, and leafy greens grow best initially.
